scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr for the same RR in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test uses one UPDATE with two identical add_rr records against an existing A RRset so the only variable is intra-message duplicate handling. Because RFC 2136 is silent on duplicate adds, implementations may either accept the message and deduplicate to a single stored RR or reject it as an error. A follow-up QUERY on the touched owner name makes the resulting rrset state observable even when the reply code is the only difference.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE and collapses duplicate adds as a benign idempotent no-op for the existing RR, leaving a single A RR in the rrset.
group_1.post_state: dup.campus.edu. has exactly one A 192.0.2.30 RR in the rrset.

group_2.behavior: Treats duplicate add_rr entries for the same RR in one UPDATE as a semantic conflict or malformed update and rejects the message.
group_2.post_state: dup.campus.edu. remains unchanged with one A 192.0.2.30 RR.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines addition semantics but does not specify how an UPDATE processor must handle duplicate add_rr entries for the same RR within one message. RFC 2181 §5.2 says an RRSet is a set, which supports deduplication, but it does not require any particular UPDATE-time behavior. Therefore implementations may differ on whether duplicate adds are silently collapsed, treated as idempotent, or rejected.
